methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $500K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Photonium is a pre-seed stage chip and semiconductor company founded by Jennifer Song and Adam Mhatre that has raised $500K to date. The broader Chips & Semiconductors sector comp universe includes 3 public peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, among them Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and serves as the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised of $500K as a proxy base value and applying a typical pre-seed to next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $1.25M. The Peer-Median method cannot be reliably applied due to insufficient comparable data points within narrow stage and valuation-tier filters—while 10 same-sector peers exist, none of the tighter peer cohorts (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) produced the minimum 3 datapoints needed for statistical credibility.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a range of $1.06M (low) to $1.44M (high), with a median estimate of $1.25M. The primary uncertainty stems from the absence of validated peer comparables at this stage and sector; a meaningful secondary check would require identifying additional pre-seed semiconductor plays or relaxing stage criteria. Until more comparable transactions surface, reliance on stage-progression methodology carries elevated estimation risk.
